{"key_event":"Third Republic defeats executive in 1877 crisis","reasoning":"Civil society rose strongly as mass male suffrage, freer press, republican parties, parliamentary institutions, and repeated elections enabled voters and organized opinion to constrain the executive, despite repression of the Commune. State power remained strong because the centralized administration survived regime collapse and was rebuilt through fiscal extraction and army reform, but defeat in 1870 and new parliamentary constraints reduced it somewhat from the late imperial peak.","society_change":1.0,"society_power":7.5,"state_change":-0.3,"state_power":8.1}
